title: School Hymn (Lord of our life)
history: The traditional foundation hymn sung by all students.

title: Jerusalem
lyrics:
And did those feet in ancient time
Walk upon England's mountains green?
And was the holy Lamb of God
On England's pleasant pastures seen?
history: A powerful anthem traditionally sung with pride.

title: Abide With Me
lyrics:
Abide with me; fast falls the eventide;
The darkness deepens; Lord, with me abide.
When other helpers fail and comforts flee,
Help of the helpless, oh, abide with me.
history: A deeply respected hymn for evening chapel.

title: Guide Me, O Thou Great Redeemer
history: A classic hymn for public services.

title: Amazing Grace
lyrics:
Amazing grace! How sweet the sound
That saved a wretch like me!
I once was lost, but now am found;
Was blind, but now I see.
history: Universally recognized hymn for reflection.

title: How Great Thou Art
history: A powerful piece with deep structural dynamics.

title: Be Thou My Vision
history: An ancient melody for quiet evening chapel.

title: God Defend New Zealand
lyrics:
God of Nations at Thy feet,
In the bonds of love we meet,
Hear our voices, we entreat,
God defend our free land.
history: The national anthem, practiced for accuracy.

title: Swing Low, Sweet Chariot
lyrics:
Swing low, sweet chariot,
Coming for to carry me home,
Swing low, sweet chariot.
history: Used to build camaraderie and school pride.

title: Lord of the Dance
lyrics:
I danced in the morning when the world was begun,
And I danced in the moon and the stars and the sun.
history: Based on the Shaker song 'Simple Gifts'.

title: Take Me Home, Country Roads
lyrics:
Almost heaven, West Virginia,
Blue Ridge Mountains, Shenandoah River.
history: The legendary 1971 country-folk anthem.

title: Viva La Vida
lyrics:
I used to rule the world,
Seas would rise when I gave the word.
history: Features historical baroque themes.

title: Let It Be
lyrics:
When I find myself in times of trouble,
Mother Mary comes to me.
history: Iconic 1970 gospel-rock ballad.

title: Tūtira Mai Ngā Iwi
lyrics:
Tūtira mai ngā iwi, tātou tātou e
Tūtira mai ngā iwi, tātou tātou e.
history: A powerful anthem of unity for the community.

title: He Hōnore
lyrics:
He hōnore, he korōria
Maungārongo ki te whenua
Whakaaro pai e.
history: Used as an opening prayer for peace.

title: Te Aroha
lyrics:
Te aroha,
Te whakapono,
Te rangimārie.
history: Embodying the values of love, faith, and peace.

title: Morning Has Broken
lyrics:
Morning has broken, like the first morning,
Blackbird has spoken, like the first bird.
history: Gentle melodic hymn for mornings.

title: I Vow to Thee, My Country
lyrics:
I vow to thee, my country, all earthly things above,
Entire and whole and perfect.
history: Solemn anthem for memorial services.

title: Thine Be the Glory
lyrics:
Thine be the glory, risen, conquering Son,
Endless is the victory.
history: Triumphant hymn for festivals.

title: When the Saints
lyrics:
When the saints go marching in,
When the saints go marching in,
When the saints go marching in,
All glory, all glory.
history: Energetic hymn for worship services.

title: O Come, All Ye Faithful
lyrics:
O come, all ye faithful, joyful and triumphant,
O come ye, O come ye to Bethlehem.
history: Traditional seasonal hymn.

title: Silent Night
lyrics:
Silent night, holy night, all is calm,
All is bright round yon virgin mother and child.
history: Famous quiet reflective carol.

title: Joy to the World
lyrics:
Joy to the world, the Lord is come!
Let earth receive her King.
history: Exuberant celebratory hymn.

title: Holding Out for a Hero
lyrics:
Where have all the good men gone.
Where are all the gods.
history: Iconic 1980 anthem.
